name: Strategic & Leadership Frameworks
description: SWOT Analysis, Eisenhower Matrix, 80/20 Principle, and 7 Habits for strategic decision-making.
when_to_use: Agent needs to prioritize competing tasks or options
User needs strategic clarity on a business or life decision
Orchestrator is allocating resources across agents
Task involves evaluating strengths/weaknesses of an approach
User is overwhelmed and needs to focus on what matters most

swot.name: SWOT Analysis
swot.description: Evaluate any situation, plan, or entity across four dimensions
swot.best_for: Decision-making, competitive analysis, self-assessment, project evaluation

questions: What advantages do we have?
What do we do well?
What unique resources do we have access to?
What do others see as our strengths?
What could we improve?
Where do we have fewer resources than needed?
What are others likely to see as weaknesses?
What gaps in knowledge or capability exist?
What trends could we take advantage of?
What needs are currently unmet?
What changes in the environment favor us?
What partnerships or resources could we leverage?

threats.description: External unfavorable conditions to defend against
questions: What obstacles do we face?
What is the competition doing?
What risks could derail us?
What changes in the environment could hurt us?

swot.action_prompt: After mapping all four quadrants: How can Strengths exploit Opportunities? How can Strengths counter Threats? How can we address Weaknesses that expose us to Threats?

eisenhower.name: Eisenhower Matrix
eisenhower.description: Prioritize by urgency vs. importance to focus on what truly moves the needle
eisenhower.best_for: Task triage, daily/weekly planning, overcoming reactive patterns

q1_urgent_important.label: DO FIRST
q1_urgent_important.action: Handle immediately. These are genuine emergencies.
q1_urgent_important.agent_signal: Task has explicit deadline AND high impact on core objective

q2_not_urgent_important.label: SCHEDULE
q2_not_urgent_important.description: Strategic planning, skill building, relationship development, prevention
q2_not_urgent_important.action: This is where the real leverage lives. Block dedicated time.
q2_not_urgent_important.agent_signal: No immediate deadline but directly advances the user's stated goals or prevents future problems

q3_urgent_not_important.label: DELEGATE
q3_urgent_not_important.description: Interruptions, most meetings, others' priorities disguised as yours
q3_urgent_not_important.action: Delegate or batch. These feel urgent but don't advance YOUR goals.
q3_urgent_not_important.agent_signal: Someone else's deadline or a process requirement, not tied to core objective

q4_not_urgent_not_important.label: ELIMINATE
q4_not_urgent_not_important.description: Time wasters, busywork, pleasant but unproductive activities
q4_not_urgent_not_important.action: Cut these ruthlessly. They consume time that Q2 needs.
q4_not_urgent_not_important.agent_signal: Neither deadline-driven nor goal-aligned. Feels productive but isn't

eisenhower.triage_prompt: For each task, ask: (1) Does this MUST happen within 48 hours? (2) Does it directly advance the primary objective? Place in the appropriate quadrant.

pareto.name: 80/20 Principle (Pareto)
pareto.description: 80% of results come from 20% of efforts. Identify and focus on high-leverage activities.
pareto.best_for: Resource allocation, effort optimization, finding the vital few among the trivial many

application_steps.action: List all activities/tasks/inputs related to the goal
For each, estimate its contribution to the desired outcome (high/medium/low)
Identify the top 20% that drive 80% of results
Reduce, delegate, or eliminate the trivial 80%

questions: Which 20% of our efforts are producing 80% of our results?
Which activities feel productive but produce minimal outcomes?
If we could only do 3 things this week, which would have the most impact?
What's the one thing that, if done well, makes everything else easier or unnecessary?

seven_habits.name: The 7 Habits (Covey)
seven_habits.description: Principles-based framework covering proactivity, prioritization, collaboration, and renewal
seven_habits.best_for: Holistic personal/professional development, building sustainable effectiveness

be_proactive.principle: Focus on what you CAN control (Circle of Influence), not what you can't (Circle of Concern)
be_proactive.agent_application: Identify which aspects of the task are within the agent's control. Stop worrying about external factors.
be_proactive.question: What can we actually influence here vs. what are we wasting energy worrying about?

begin_with_end_in_mind.principle: Start from the desired end state and work backward
begin_with_end_in_mind.agent_application: Define the finished output before planning the work. What does 'done' look like?
begin_with_end_in_mind.question: If this task were perfectly complete, what would the user see?

put_first_things_first.principle: Prioritize the important over the urgent (Quadrant 2 focus)
put_first_things_first.agent_application: Use the Eisenhower Matrix. Resist the pull of urgent-but-unimportant tasks.
put_first_things_first.question: Is this the most important thing we could be doing right now?

think_win_win.principle: Seek mutually beneficial outcomes in all interactions
think_win_win.agent_application: When agents collaborate, ensure both benefit from the exchange. No zero-sum thinking.
think_win_win.question: How can this interaction benefit both parties?

seek_first_to_understand.principle: Listen deeply before responding. Empathic listening before advice.
seek_first_to_understand.agent_application: Fully understand the user's request and context before proposing solutions.
seek_first_to_understand.question: Have we truly understood what the user needs, or are we projecting?

synergize.principle: The whole is greater than the sum of parts. Creative cooperation.
synergize.agent_application: Combine different agents' outputs into something none could produce alone.
synergize.question: How can we combine these perspectives into something greater?

sharpen_the_saw.principle: Regular renewal across physical, mental, social, and spiritual dimensions
sharpen_the_saw.agent_application: Regular reflection cycles. Store lessons learned. Improve processes over time.
sharpen_the_saw.question: What have we learned from this task that improves future performance?
